``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Time actually served by an individual prisoner is determined by the judicial maximum sentence minus any conduct-based credits. There is no provision for good-time credits that accrue continuously over time merely because people in prison have avoided serious disciplinary violations. people in prison may accumulate credits only through active participation in programs or other affirmative “meritorious” conduct. For “serious violent offenses” the maximum credit is four days per month. people in prison serving sentences for “nonviolent offenses” (defined as crimes other than “serious violent offenses”) can earn as much as thirty days per month.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 23%, changing from 6,659 in 2010 to 5,154 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2017, prison releases decreased 2%, dropping from 3,572 in 2010 to 3,492 in 2017. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::

``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2017, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in New Mexico. The largest change was for other or unknown offenses, which decreased by 100%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
